document: Thus in R v Morris offences which could not have been laid earlier become available. Thus in R v Morris (1867) LR 1 CCR 90, the accused had originally been convicted of assault. document: DPP v Doot The offence of conspiracy cannot exist without t he agreement, consent or combination of two or more persons. See also: HKSAR v Lee Wai Ki & Ors [2001] 2 HKLRD 610, [2001] HKCU 582 (CFI). A conspiracy does not end with the making of the agreement, it continues so long as there are two or more parties to it intending to carry out the design: see DPP v Doot [1973] AC 807 (HL). 1047 Dangerous Drugs Ordinance (Cap 134) [39.01] bwpageid:: 1047:: bwservice::::

(d) Withdrawal from the enterprise [15-99] The rules applicable to withdrawal from secondary liability remain complex. Pre-Jogee 183 cases suggest that a withdrawal is valid where it takes place before the principal commits the offence, and before the act of assistance by the accomplice is effective.
